author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm XXV

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To the end, a psalm of David himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Christ speaks, but in the person of the Church: for what is said has reference rather to the Christian People turned unto God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Unto Thee, O Lord, have I lift up my soul” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 1): with spiritual longing have I lift up the soul, that was trodden down on the earth with carnal longings.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O my God, in Thee I trust, I shall not be ashamed” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O my God, from trusting in myself I was brought even to this weakness of the flesh; and I who on abandoning God wished to be as God, fearing death from the smallest insect, was in derision ashamed for my pride; now, therefore, “in Thee I trust, I shall not be ashamed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And let not my enemies mock me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And let them not mock me, who by ensnaring me with serpent-like and secret suggestions, and prompting me with “Well done, well done,” have brought me down to this.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For all that wait upon Thee shall not be confounded” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let them be confounded who do vain things unrighteously.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let them be confounded who act unrighteously for the acquiring things that pass away.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Make Thy ways, O Lord, known to me, and teach me Thy paths” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 4): not those which are broad, and lead the many to destruction; but Thy paths, narrow, and known to few, teach Thou me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In Thy truth guide me:” avoiding error.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And teach me:” for by myself I know nothing, but falsehood.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For Thou art the God of my salvation; and for Thee have I waited all the day” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For dismissed by Thee from Paradise, and having taken my journey into a far country, I cannot by myself return, unless Thou meetest the wanderer: for my return hath throughout the whole tract of this world’s time waited for Thy mercy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Remember Thy compassions, O Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Remember the works of Thy mercy, O Lord; for men deem of Thee as though Thou hadst forgotten.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And that Thy mercies are from eternity.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And remember this, that Thy mercies are from eternity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For Thou never wast without them, who hast subjected even sinful man to vanity indeed, but in hope; and not deprived him of so many and great consolations of Thy creation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Remember not the offences of my youth and of my ignorance” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The offences of my presumptuous boldness and of my ignorance reserve not for vengeance, but let them be as if forgotten by Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “According to Thy mercy, be mindful of me, O God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be mindful indeed of me, not according to the anger of which I am worthy, but according to Thy mercy which is worthy of Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For Thy goodness, O Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not for my deservings, but for Thy goodness, O Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Gracious and upright is the Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Lord is gracious, since even sinners and the ungodly He so pitied, as to forgive all that is past; but the Lord is upright too, who after the mercy of vocation and pardon, which is of grace without merit, will require merits meet for the last judgment.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Wherefore He will establish a law for them that fail in the way.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He hath first bestowed mercy to bring them into the way.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He will guide the meek in judgment.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He will guide the meek, and will not confound in the judgment those that follow His will, and do not, in withstanding It, prefer their own.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The gentle He will teach His ways” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He will teach His ways, not to those that desire to run before, as if they were better able to rule themselves; but to those who do not exalt the neck, nor lift the heel, when the easy yoke and the light burden is laid upon them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “All the ways of the Lord are mercy and truth” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what ways will He teach them, but mercy wherein He is placable, and truth wherein He is incorrupt?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whereof He hath exhibited the one in forgiving sins, the other in judging deserts.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And therefore “all the ways of the Lord” are the two advents of the Son of God, the one in mercy, the other in judgment.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He then attaineth unto Him holding on His ways, who seeing himself freed by no deserts of his own, lays pride aside, and henceforward bewares of the severity of His trial, having experienced the clemency of His help.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “To them that seek His testament and His testimonies.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they understand the Lord as merciful at His first advent, and as the Judge at His second, who in meekness and gentleness seek His testament, when with His Own Blood He redeemed us to a new life; and in the Prophets and Evangelists, His testimonies.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For Thy Name’s sake, O Lord, Thou wilt be favourable to my sin; for it is manifold” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast not only forgiven my sins, which I committed before I believed; but also to my sin, which is manifold, since even in the way there is no lack of stumbling, Thou wilt be made favourable by the sacrifice of a troubled spirit.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who is the man that feareth the Lord?” from which fear he begins to come to wisdom.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He shall establish a law for him in the way, which he hath chosen” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He shall establish a law for him in the way, which in his freedom he has taken, that he may not sin now with impunity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “His soul shall dwell in good, and his seed shall, by inheritance, possess the earth” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And his work shall possess the stable inheritance of a renewed body.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord is the stay of them that fear Him” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Fear seems to belong to the weak, but the Lord is the stay of them that fear Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And the Name of the Lord, which hath been glorified throughout the whole world, is a stay to them that fear Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And His testament, that it may be manifested unto them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And He maketh His testament to be manifested unto them, for the Gentiles and the bounds of the earth are Christ’s inheritance.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Mine eyes are ever unto the Lord; for He shall pluck my feet out of the snare” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nor would I fear the dangers of earth, while I look not upon the earth: for He upon whom I look, will pluck my feet out of the snare.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Look upon me, and have mercy upon me; for I am single and poor” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For I am a single people, keeping the lowliness of Thy single Church, which no schisms or heresies possess.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The tribulations of my heart have been multiplied” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The tribulations of my heart have been multiplied by the abounding of iniquity and the waxing cold of love.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O bring Thou me out of my necessities.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Since I must needs bear this, that by enduring unto the end I may be saved, bring Thou me out of my necessities.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “See my humility and my travail” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See my humility, whereby I never, in the boast of righteousness, break off from unity; and my travail, wherein I bear with the unruly ones that are mingled with me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And forgive all my sins.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And, propitiated by these sacrifices, forgive all my sins, not those only of youth and my ignorance before I believed, but those also which, living now by faith, I commit through infirmity, or the darkness of this life.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Consider mine enemies, how they are multiplied” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For not only without, but even within, in the Church’s very communion, they are not wanting.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And with an unrighteous hate they hate me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And they hate me who love them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Keep my soul, and deliver me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Keep my soul, that I turn not aside to imitate them; and draw me out from the confusion wherein they are mingled with me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let me not be confounded, for I have put my trust in Thee” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let me not be confounded, if haply they rise up against me: for not in myself, but in Thee have I put my trust.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The innocent and the upright have cleaved to me, for I have waited for Thee, O Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The innocent and the upright, not in bodily presence only, as the evil, are mingled with me, but in the agreement of the heart in the same innocence and uprightness cleave to me: for I have not fallen away to imitate the evil; but I have waited for Thee, expecting the winnowing of Thy last harvest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Redeem Israel, O God, out of all his troubles” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Redeem Thy people, O God,” whom Thou hast prepared to see Thee, out of his troubles, not those only which he bears without, but those also which he bears within.
